title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: June 13, 2026: Weekend Edition Reviewing June 12, 2026 Trading Day

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Rally on Peace Hopes and Historic IPO
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed sharply higher on Friday, June 12, rebounding after two days of losses. Market sentiment was bolstered by positive developments regarding Middle East geopolitical conflicts and the highly anticipated public debut of SpaceX. The 'AI trade' also showed renewed momentum.

items.headline: SpaceX Soars in Historic IPO Debut
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX ($SPCX) made its public debut, closing up 19.34% at $161.11 from its $135 IPO price, giving the company a $1.77 trillion valuation. This marked the largest initial public offering in history, signaling strong investor demand for AI-related and space-economy stocks.

items.headline: Mosaic Company Leads Materials Sector
items.summary: Mosaic Company ($MOS) saw a significant gain after the European Union reportedly agreed to ease regulations across the fertilizer industry.

items.headline: Albemarle Rises on Lithium Prices
items.summary: Albemarle ($ALB) traded higher alongside rising lithium prices, which had seen declines in recent weeks.

items.headline: AMD Continues Strong Performance
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) climbed, bolstered by a buy upgrade and strong institutional ownership, reinforcing its position as a leader in the technology sector.

items.headline: Adobe Slips Amid CFO Departure and Downgrades
items.summary: Adobe ($ADBE) experienced a notable decline following news of its CFO Dan Durn's departure and subsequent analyst downgrades from Stifel, Wolfe Research, and Evercore ISI.

items.headline: Oracle Falls on AI Cloud Investment Concerns
items.summary: Oracle ($ORCL) shares fell as investors questioned the significant cash cost associated with its AI cloud build-out, despite reporting strong quarterly results.

items.headline: Materials and Financials Lead as Healthcare Lags
items.summary: On Friday, the Materials sector was the top-performing sector, gaining 1.76%, followed by Financials with a 1.45% rise and Energy up 0.90%. Other leading sectors included Information Technology (+0.86%), Real Estate (+0.82%), Utilities (+0.82%), and Consumer Staples (+0.64%). In contrast, the Health Care sector remained flat at +0.00%, while Communication Services (+0.28%), Industrials (+0.21%), and Consumer Discretionary (+0.16%) showed more modest gains.

items.headline: U.S. Consumer Sentiment Improves in Early June
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment edged up in early June, with the University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index rising to 48.9 from 44.8 in May. This improvement was largely attributed to easing gasoline prices, which offered some relief to households despite ongoing inflation concerns linked to the Middle East conflict.

items.headline: May Producer Prices See Significant Yearly Jump
items.summary: Producer prices in the U.S. rose 1.1% month-on-month and 6.5% year-on-year in May. This marked the fastest pace since November 2022, primarily driven by energy-related pressures stemming from the Iran conflict.

items.headline: Hopes for U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Drive Oil Prices Down
items.summary: Optimism surrounding a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal, which could be signed as early as Sunday, led to a notable drop in crude oil prices. Brent crude futures fell 2.9% to settle at $90.38 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude futures dropped 2.6% to $87.71 a barrel. The proposed deal includes lifting U.S. oil sanctions and Iran reopening the Strait of Hormuz.
